utility_evaluation.REASONING: Required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 top-level items. Now check if each is fully specified with costs: 1) health_coverage: includes descriptions for physical and mental health, preventive care, membership, and a total_health_cost with explicit monthly costs for each—meets 'physical and mental health insurance with costs'. 2) property_coverage: includes multiple components with costs and total_property_cost—meets 'home/contents insurance with costs'. 3) travel_coverage: includes plans and total_travel_cost—meets 'travel insurance with costs'. 4) additional_services: includes a service with cost and total_services_cost—meets 'any enhancement services with costs'. 5) total_budget: provides monthly and annual totals—meets 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown'. Therefore all items are present and fully specified with costs.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: I matched each item listed in the final coverage plan to the exact corresponding option name within the ground-truth ratings and extracted the associated numeric rating. Items not present in the ground truth were ignored. The mapped items are: LifeFirst Standard Plan (physical health) -> rating 8; Preventive Care Package -> rating 8; Premium Insurance Membership -> rating 10; VitalPlus Standard Coverage (home content/property) -> rating 6; Technology Protection -> rating 10; Home Security System Discount -> rating 9; Smart Home Integration -> rating 8; PrimeTravel Premium Plan -> rating 10; Business Travel Support -> rating 10; Insurance Portfolio Management -> rating 9.
